mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that helps you identify vulnerabilities in your container images and running workloads. It is particularly useful for organizations looking to enhance their security posture by integrating vulnerability scanning into their CI/CD pipelines. Key trade-offs include the need for additional resources and potential performance overhead during scans.

steps.title: Add the Aqua Security Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Aqua Security Helm repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install the Trivy Operator.

```bash

helm repo add aqua https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Aqua Security Helm charts repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Installs the Trivy Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Lists all pods in the 'trivy-system' namespace to verify the installation.

steps.title: Create a sample Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Trivy Custom Resource (CR) to initiate vulnerability scanning.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    kind: Pod
    name: example-pod
    namespace: default
EOF

```

> Creates a sample VulnerabilityReport CR.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: example-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    kind: Pod
    name: example-pod
    namespace: default
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all vulnerability reports and configurations associated with the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important configurations. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.phase}'

helm upgrade trivy-operator aqua/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.23.0

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Check the status of the pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n trivy-system -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.phase}'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create resource: Forbidden'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service account used by the Trivy Operator does not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the necessary RBAC permissions by applying the required Role and RoleBinding.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No matching resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified resource in the Custom Resource does not exist.

**Fix:**
Verify that the resource name and namespace in the Custom Resource are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors and ensure all required configurations are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is unreachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network configuration and ensure the Kubernetes cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for scanning additional resource types and improved performance of vulnerability scans.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required when upgrading from v0.22.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use 'kubectl get pods -n trivy-system' to verify.
codeSnippets.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace and the created VulnerabilityReport should show a status of 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.25 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
